subject: english
source: research/10-skill-tree-blueprints.md §1.2 Branch E3; KS2 Y5/Y6 statutory spelling appendix (GOV.UK).
notes: Each item: correct = real English word; distractors = plausible misspellings of the same target (not real words where avoidable). pronounceWord mirrors correct for the 'hear the word' affordance in question.js.

items.prompt: The car was ___ in the driveway.
Which ___ you prefer, tea or coffee?
She had no idea what to do ___.
The pirate buried his ___ on the island.
The weather is much too ___ today.
He sat ___ to me on the bench.
She wrote her name on the piece of ___.
The head teacher is called the ___.
It is against my ___ to lie.
He paid her a lovely ___ on her dress.
The wine will ___ the meal nicely.
I ___ you to take the early train.
We can no longer ___ the delay.
He had been ___ looking all morning.
